segments.text: O God, do not remain silent; do not turn a deaf ear, do not stand aloof, O God.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: See how your enemies growl, how your foes rear their heads.
With cunning they conspire against your people; they plot against those you cherish.
Come, let us destroy them as a nation, so that Israel's name is remembered no more.
segments.speaker: Enemies

segments.text: With one mind they plot together; they form an alliance against you—
the tents of Edom and the Ishmaelites, of Moab and the Hagrites,
Byblos, Ammon and Amalek, Philistia, with the people of Tyre.
Even Assyria has joined them to reinforce Lot's descendants.
Do to them as you did to Midian, as you did to Sisera and Jabin at the river Kishon,
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: who perished at Endor and became like dung on the ground.
Make their nobles like Oreb and Zeeb, all their princes like Zebah and Zalmunna,
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Let us take possession of the pasturelands of God.
segments.speaker: Enemies

segments.text: Make them like tumbleweed, my God, like chaff before the wind.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: As fire consumes the forest or a flame sets the mountains ablaze,
so pursue them with your tempest and terrify them with your storm.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Cover their faces with shame, Lord, so that they will seek your name.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: May they ever be ashamed and dismayed; may they perish in disgrace.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Let them know that you, whose name is the Lord— that you alone are the Most High over all the earth.
segments.speaker: David
